tweets.text: Further elaboration on this - given it looks like another VC funding cycle is starting and I don't want founders to go down the 'market standard' path of getting fucked over by VCs. If you are fundraising or know anyone fundraising, please fucking bookmark and share this. When you raise money via a SAFE + Token Warrant, for a project that intends to have a token, the most important provision in your fundraising documents is the definition of 'Portion' in your token warrant. This defines how many tokens your investor gets in the event there is a TGE and you must negotiate this. There are broadly, two versions of the deal structure. See the attached image. In the first structure, your investors get a percentage of your total supply. So if your investors invested 1m at 10m valuation cap (lets not account for potential dilution rn), the intention is they would get 10% of your total supply. So if you TGE with 1 billion tokens, your investors would get 100 million tokens. In the second structure, your investors get a percentage of the 'company supply'. So if your investors invest 1m at 10m valuation cap, what they get is 10% of whatever supply you allocate to team. So if you allocate 30% to 'team', the investor would get 30 million tokens (10% of 300 million). Investors will try to tell you that the first structure is 'standard', because they got used to it in the 2022 venture cycle and because its very investor favored. You need to push back on this because its fucking retarded. When an investor invests in you, what should happen is that their outcome should be tied to yours. When you sell them '10%' of your equity, the logical position should be that you are selling them 10% of your economics. i.e. if you as the project team exits for 90m, they get 10m. When you allow this mentally ill structure into your funding round, what is happening is that you are getting a far lower 'true val' than you are expecting. When you TGE, you aren't going to allocate 90% of tokens to team and 10% to investors. You are realistically only going to allocate 20-30% to team, because the rest should be used for protocol growth. So why the fuck would you allocate 10% to investors? With this provision, you think you have only sold 10% of your economics, but you have really sold something like 33%, because your investor has an outsized allocation in the protocol economics vis your team! Please for the love of Christ push back on this retarded framing of selling a fixed percentage of total supply to VCs. Image attached to demonstrate how the two different positions work. I'll write a longer essay when I'm sober, I'm like 7 drinks in.
tweets.summary: Further elaboration on this - given it looks like another VC funding cycle is starting and I don't want founders to go down the 'market standard'…

tweets.text: RT @amandatums: This story gets DOJ’s position wrong, gets Section 1960 wrong, and gets both Senator Cortez Masto’s amendment and the Grassley compromise wrong. I’d be impressed if it wasn’t so disappointing to see more unsupported anti-Clarity reporting. Putting aside the fact that @BrendanPedersen does not include recent, directly-on-point, publicly reported DOJ statements from June 23 saying the exact opposite of the supposed DOJ email cited here (see below), the bigger issue is that the article and the DOJ statements cited within do not accurately cite the criminal code. Money laundering is prohibited in 18 USC 1956 and 1957; operating an unlicensed money transmitting business is covered in 18 USC 1960. The BRCA clarifies the application of “money transmitting” for the BSA and 1960 and says nothing at all about money laundering or 1956 or 1957. Further breakdown in replies
tweets.summary: RT @amandatums: This story gets DOJ’s position wrong, gets Section 1960 wrong, and gets both Senator Cortez Masto’s amendment and the Grassley…

tweets.text: Unironically, Ukraine. Set up in a contested town / city that’s held by Ukraine atm, ideally one that was previously blitzed by Russia but retaken and has limited strategic value. Set up a drone unit of non-NATO citizens and have them support the war effort deep underground in a limited but not immaterial capacity. NATO citizens should support but not be in combat roles. Build rapport with locals. Europe / USA has mostly lost interest in war and Russia won’t want to inflict NATO citizen casualties to reinvigorate them. Stockpile drones and weaponry in the chaos of war. Pray a ceasefire and peace treaty is achieved. Flood investment into the town / city after peace is achieved. Align with local government etc. Rebuild parts of the city and claim ownership under local laws. Lobby / bribe your way into de facto SEZ status, whilst ostensibly integrating your drone unit into Ukrainian military. Do not surrender entire weapons stockpile. National service for all NS new joiners, six months of combat drone training. Secretly build a Samson Option with the weapons stockpile you hid. It’s not nuclear but enough to blow up key infra in the host country. Have a few brave volunteers be caught installing your mutually assured destruction apparatus. Denounce them terrorists and “execute” them. Hand over the “plans” and fully “cooperate” with the Ukrainian government. We do not support terrorist action and we are guests under the Ukrainians sovereignty. Now you have a friendly host nation that will always, have at the back of its mind, the possibility that a Samson Option exists. You have your own military. You have rights enshrined in a SEZ arrangement. You can borrow the international relations and the military of the host nation. Welcome to the network state.

tweets.text: The information released this evening by the White House regarding the 2020 data breach by China and potential vulnerabilities in voting machines is alarming and deeply concerning. Even more troubling are reports that officials within the federal bureaucracy kept both the President and Congress in the dark about this unprecedented breach. Every American who believes in representative democracy should expect transparency and accountability. Congress deserves a full accounting of what occurred and any actions the Biden administration took in response, including allegations that information was withheld or concealed. The American people must have confidence in their government, and failures of this magnitude undermine that trust. I appreciate @POTUS bringing these allegations to the public’s attention, and I will continue seeking additional information to better understand the full scope of what occurred. We must protect our elections and pass the SAVE America Act.

tweets.text: "It seems like he's bringing some of the product guys at Coinbase down a notch and back into the community" Taylor on why Cobie's ground floor approach matters at Coinbase "When Cobie joined Coinbase originally I could see it, two things could happen. First is that he just gets so disgusted by Coinbase and their ivory tower shit and just immediately gets super disenchanted and gives up. The other is that he powers through and brings his unique sort of, the way he approaches things" "He's never up in the ivory tower, he is always on the ground floor, he's always listening to people, bringing that to Coinbase and Coinbase employees. I think he's still doing that" "He's been quieter on Twitter lately, but back listening, and if they can do that successfully, I think it's massively important"

tweets.text: What in the actual hell? The grift in Washington & MA is mind blowing. @AyannaPressley goes from a net worth of about $12,500 before being elected to over $8M in 7 years. Mind you, her and her husband achieve wealth with her making $174K salary - and maintaining two residences in two of the most expensive housing markets in the country. Her husband - Conan Harris - served 10 years in prison on a drug trafficking charge. However, He had the great foresight to found Conan Harris & Associates, a consulting firm, THREE DAYS BEFORE Pressley took office in 2019. Recently, Conan Harris and his team were selected to build the roughly $2 billion Springfield courthouse project - with a 40-year lease deal - with the property reverting back to his company at the end of the lease. 🤯 His connections clearly paid off considering he beat out local Springfield bidders to win this enormous contract. Maybe I’m being too cynical and he truly was the most qualified bidder to build a 250,000 square foot state-of-the-art courthouse. 🤔 After all, I hear prison has a really good “wood shop” class nowadays. But just before I was about to comment on Conan Harris, I read this 💩 👇 We truly live in the corruption era. In fact, the ONLY thing bipartisan in Washington is corruption.

tweets.text: RT @valkenburgh: Something has shifted in the last few years... Digital rights groups have become very shy about cryptocurrency work even where regulation of those technologies directly impacts principles like code as speech and warrantless surveillance online. Crypto groups have become increasingly focused on institutional and financial markets questions and a bit less dedicated to the original mission: replacing government and corporate infrastructure with free and open peer-to-peer systems. We want to talk more about the peer-to-peer part and maybe earn some stronger alliances with the larger freedom tech movement. As a small first step, we're launching a new space to talk about tech authoritarianism and the peer-to-peer alternatives: Peer-to-Peer from Coin Center. Please subscribe and share.

tweets.text: RT @NatCryptoAssoc: The Clarity Act is still working its way through Congress. Here is what you should know. 💡 67 million people in this country hold crypto today. The Clarity Act aims to fix that by defining whether a digital asset falls under the SEC or the CFTC, helping pave the way for more comprehensive consumer protections.
